**Dieter Reiter** (born 19 May 1958) is a German politician and the [mayor](/wiki/List_of_mayors_of_Munich "List of mayors of Munich") of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich").[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is a member of the [Social Democratic Party](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_of_Germany "Social Democratic Party of Germany") (SPD).

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dieter_Reiter&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Reiter was born in [Rain, Swabia](/wiki/Rain,_Swabia "Rain, Swabia"), a small town in [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria"). He studied at the Fachhochschule für öffentliche Verwaltung und Rechtspflege in [Hof](/wiki/Hof,_Bavaria "Hof, Bavaria"), where he finished 1981. On 30 March 2014, he was elected mayor of Munich with 56.7% of the votes.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He succeeded [Christian Ude](/wiki/Christian_Ude "Christian Ude"), who served as mayor from 1993 to 2014. Reiter was SPD delegate to the [Federal Convention](/wiki/Federal_Convention_(Germany) "Federal Convention (Germany)") for the purpose of electing the [President of Germany](/wiki/President_of_Germany "President of Germany") in 2017.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2021, Reiter proposed illuminating the stadium for [UEFA Euro 2020](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_2020 "UEFA Euro 2020") in rainbow colors to protest the [Hungarian anti-LGBT law](/wiki/Hungarian_anti-LGBT_law "Hungarian anti-LGBT law"), but this was rejected by [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On March 22, 2026, Reiter lost the mayoral elections in Munich against [Dominik Krause](/wiki/Dominik_Krause "Dominik Krause").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Other activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dieter_Reiter&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Other activities")]

### Corporate boards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dieter_Reiter&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Corporate boards")]

* [FC Bayern](/wiki/FC_Bayern_Munich "FC Bayern Munich"), member of the advisory board[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Stadtwerke München](/wiki/Stadtwerke_M%C3%BCnchen "Stadtwerke München") (SWM), ex officio chairman of the supervisory board
* [Munich Airport](/wiki/Munich_Airport "Munich Airport"), ex officio member of the supervisory board
* [Messe München](/wiki/Messe_M%C3%BCnchen "Messe München"), ex officio member of the supervisory board

### Non-profit organizations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dieter_Reiter&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Non-profit organizations")]

* [LMU Munich](/wiki/LMU_Munich "LMU Munich"), member of the board of trustees[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* [Technical University of Munich](/wiki/Technical_University_of_Munich "Technical University of Munich") (TUM), member of the board of trustees
* [Deutsches Museum](/wiki/Deutsches_Museum "Deutsches Museum"), member of the board of trustees
* [Ifo Institute for Economic Research](/wiki/Ifo_Institute_for_Economic_Research "Ifo Institute for Economic Research"), member of the board of trustees[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* [ver.di](/wiki/Ver.di "Ver.di"), member

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dieter_Reiter&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Reiter has been married twice and is the father of a son from his first marriage. His second wife, Petra, has two children of her own from a previous relationship.
